title: hieracium caesium subsp. farrense
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. farrense, also known as Farren's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with yellow daisy-like flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is said to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues and reducing fever. The Farren's Hawkweed is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in salves and ointments to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds.
